tagline: From scanned ECG documents to EMR-ready clinical data in 7 steps

steps.title: Classify Inputs
steps.body: Automatically detect whether each file is a scanned PDF, image, CSV export, WFDB, or EDF waveform — and route to the right pipeline.
steps.visualContent: PDF / Image / CSV / Waveform → classifier → pipeline branch

steps.title: Image Preprocessing
steps.body: Enhance scans, correct skew, remove grid lines, and reduce noise before OCR on 12-lead printouts and Holter summaries.
steps.visualContent: Preprocessing chain for low-quality clinical scans.

steps.title: Clinical OCR & Extraction
steps.body: Multilingual OCR plus rule-based and semantic extraction pulls intervals, rates, and segments from document text.
steps.visualContent: QRS, RR, QT, ST, heart rate where present in source.
steps.workflowStepRef: Steps 3–4 — OCR and Clinical Field Extraction

steps.title: Normalize for EMR
steps.body: Map naming variations, validate clinical ranges, and output standardized schema ready for EMR or analytics pipelines.

steps.title: Continuous Learning
steps.body: Offline training on diverse ECG datasets improves accuracy and language coverage over time without blocking production ingest.
steps.visualContent: Feedback loop from validated outputs to model refresh.
